name: Luno Cryptocurrency Exchange API
what_it_does: Regulated cryptocurrency exchange REST and WebSocket API focused on Africa, Southeast Asia, and Europe — enabling traders and developers to access Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP, and other cryptocurrency trading pairs, with HTTP Basic authentication, official SDKs in multiple languages, and compliance with local regulations in Nigeria, South Africa, Malaysia, and other emerging markets.
use_cases: Trading Bitcoin and Ethereum via Luno REST API for African and Southeast Asian markets
Integrating ZAR (South African Rand) crypto pairs via Luno API for local market exposure
Managing cryptocurrency wallets via Luno API for Africa-focused fintech applications
Accessing NGN (Nigerian Naira) trading pairs via Luno API for Nigerian market integration
Building crypto on/off-ramp solutions via Luno API for emerging market fintech products
not_for: Wide altcoin trading (Luno supports limited pairs: BTC, ETH, XRP, LTC, BCH, USDC, USDT, XLM)
US users (Luno does not operate in the US)
High-frequency or derivatives trading (Luno is primarily spot exchange for retail)
best_when: A fintech developer targeting African or Southeast Asian markets needs a regulated cryptocurrency exchange API with local currency support (ZAR, NGN, MYR) and compliance with local regulations.
avoid_when: You need wide altcoin selection, derivatives, US market access, or institutional-grade services.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic auth: API key as username, API secret as password. Authorization: Basic base64(api_key:api_secret). Keys generated in Luno account settings with permission levels: Read-Only, Orders, Withdrawals. No OAuth2. No webhooks. REST API at api.luno.com. Simple and clean auth — no HMAC signing required.

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access free; maker 0.10% / taker 0.10% (volume discounts available)
pricing.notes: UK company (London) with operations in South Africa, Nigeria, Malaysia, Indonesia, Uganda, Zambia, Zimbabwe, Italy, France, Spain, Portugal. Founded 2013 by Marcus Swanepoel and Timothy Stranex (South African). Acquired by Digital Currency Group (DCG) 2020. South African focus — one of the largest African crypto platforms. 10M+ customers. Supported currencies: ZAR, NGN, MYR, IDR, EUR, GBP, UGX, ZMW, ZWL. Simple HTTP Basic auth is a developer-friendly choice. Limited pair count — major coins only. Regulation-first approach: licensed in multiple jurisdictions. Blockchain education emphasis (Luno Learn).

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; standard rate limits apply

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HTTP Basic auth (simplest scheme). 6 official SDKs. African/SE Asian focus. Limited pairs. UK/ZA/NG regulated. No webhooks. No HMAC signing. DCG acquisition 2020.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Order reads idempotent by order ID. Cancel idempotent by order ID. Order creation not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: HTTP Basic auth: api_key as username, api_secret as password — simple but log security caution
Limited pair selection: BTC, ETH, XRP, LTC, BCH, USDC, USDT, XLM (major coins only)
Local currency pairs: ZAR, NGN, MYR, IDR — check supported pairs per currency
US market not served — Luno operates in Africa, SE Asia, Europe
Acquired by DCG (Digital Currency Group) 2020 — same parent as Genesis Trading (insolvency risk context)
No webhooks — must poll for balance and order updates

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HTTP Basic auth (simple — no HMAC signing but credentials transmitted each request). API key permissions (read/orders/withdrawals). UK FCA + South African FSCA regulated. GDPR compliant. DCG parent (Genesis bankruptcy 2023) — vendor financial risk context. Cryptocurrency financial data — high value target. No OAuth2. Log sanitization required (Basic auth in headers).
